subject: leaf certificate validity
truth: Generated leaf certificate validity is reduced from 199 to 197 days to stay under Chromium's 200-day limit with the 2-day notBefore backdate.
quote: Reduce generated leaf certificate validity from 199 to 197 days so the 2-day notBefore backdate remains below Chromium's 200-day limit.

subject: CERT_EXPIRY constant
truth: Stop relying on certificates being valid for more than 199 days; the CERT_EXPIRY constant has been reduced to 199 days.
quote: Reduce `CERT_EXPIRY` to 199 days.

subject: content-encoding compression algorithms
truth: Stop expecting default content-encoding compression to use slower/higher-compression settings; all algorithms now use fastest settings by default, which changes output size and performance characteristics.
quote: Switch all content-encoding compression algorithms to use fastest settings by default.

subject: htpasswd file parser
old: passlib-based htpasswd parser supporting many hash formats
new: custom htpasswd parser supporting only bcrypt and SHA-1
truth: Stop using passlib-based htpasswd file parsing; the new parser only supports bcrypt and SHA-1 hashing, other hash formats will fail.
quote: Replace `htpasswd` file parser with a custom implementation to migrate off unmaintained `passlib` dependency. The new parser only supports bcrypt and SHA-1 hashing.

from_fact: passlib-based htpasswd parser supporting many hash formats
custom htpasswd parser supporting only bcrypt and sha-1

subject: Docker base image
truth: Docker images are now built with Debian Trixie instead of the previous Debian release.
quote: Docker images are now build with Debian Trixie.

subject: JSON contentview escaping
old: non-ASCII characters were escaped in JSON contentview
new: non-ASCII characters are no longer escaped in JSON contentview
truth: Non-ASCII characters in the JSON contentview are no longer escaped, which may change displayed output for non-ASCII payloads.
quote: Do not escape non-ascii characters in the JSON contentview.

from_fact: non-ascii characters were escaped in json contentview
non-ascii characters are no longer escaped in json contentview

subject: DNS proxy mode addon hook timing
old: user-provided addons triggered after DNS resolution
new: user-provided addons trigger before DNS resolution
truth: In DNS proxy mode, user-provided addons now trigger before DNS resolution has taken place, which may break addons that relied on DNS resolution having already occurred.
quote: In DNS proxy mode, user-provided addons now trigger before DNS resolution has taken place.

from_fact: user-provided addons triggered after dns resolution
user-provided addons trigger before dns resolution

subject: contentview API return type
old: Contentviews returned structured data with syntax highlighting info
new: Contentviews now return a plain `str` with the prettified data; syntax highlighting is signaled off-band
truth: Stop relying on contentviews to return syntax-highlighted structured output; they now return a plain string and highlighting is handled separately via tree-sitter.
quote: Contentviews now return a plain `str` with the prettified data. Syntax highlighting is now signaled off-band (and based on [tree-sitter]).

from_fact: contentviews returned structured data with syntax highlighting info
contentviews now return a plain `str` with the prettified data; syntax highlighting is signaled off-band

subject: gRPC and Protobuf contentviews
new: A single interactive contentview supporting both known proto definitions and unknown protos
truth: Stop using the old separate gRPC and Protobuf contentviews; they have been replaced by one interactive contentview that handles both known and unknown protos.
quote: Replace the existing gRPC and Protobuf contentviews with an interactive contentview that supports both existing proto definitions and completely unknown protos.

from_fact: a single interactive contentview supporting both known proto definitions and unknown protos

subject: mitmproxy.dns.Message
truth: Stop using mitmproxy.dns.Message, use mitmproxy.dns.DNSMessage instead.
quote: `mitmproxy.dns.Message` has been renamed to `mitmproxy.dns.DNSMessage`

subject: mitmweb API authentication
old: mitmweb API did not require an authentication token by default
new: mitmweb API now requires an authentication token by default
truth: mitmweb's API now requires an authentication token by default; you must provide a token when making API requests.
quote: mitmweb's API now requires an authentication token by default.

from_fact: mitmweb api did not require an authentication token by default
mitmweb api now requires an authentication token by default

subject: mitmweb password protection
truth: The `web_password` option replaces the randomly-generated token authentication; use `web_password` to set a fixed secret that survives restarts.
quote: The `web_password` option replaces the randomly-generated token authentication with a fixed secret that survives mitmproxy restarts.

subject: mitmweb xsrf_token cookie
new: xsrf_token cookie is now HttpOnly; SameSite=Strict
truth: mitmweb's xsrf_token cookie is now HttpOnly and SameSite=Strict, which may break scripts that read or send this cookie from JavaScript.
quote: mitmweb's xsrf_token cookie is now HttpOnly; SameSite=Strict.

from_fact: xsrf_token cookie is now httponly; samesite=strict

subject: Python version requirement
truth: mitmproxy now requires Python 3.12 or above; running on Python 3.11 or older will fail to start.
quote: mitmproxy now requires Python 3.12 or above.

subject: Filter expression case sensitivity
old: Filter expressions were case-sensitive by default
new: Filter expressions are now case-insensitive by default
truth: All filter expressions are now case-insensitive by default; to restore case-sensitive behaviour set MITMPROXY_CASE_SENSITIVE_FILTERS=1 environment variable.
quote: All filter expressions are now case-insensitive by default. Users can opt into case-sensitive filters by setting MITMPROXY_CASE_SENSITIVE_FILTERS=1 as an environment variable.

from_fact: filter expressions were case-sensitive by default
filter expressions are now case-insensitive by default

subject: Filter expression lowercasing in block_list addon
truth: The block_list addon no longer lowercases filter expressions; existing filters that relied on automatic lowercasing may break.
quote: Remove filter expression lowercasing in block_list addon

subject: Status code check in blocklist add-on
truth: The blocklist add-on no longer checks status codes; existing configurations that relied on status code filtering in blocklist will stop working.
quote: Remove check for status codes in the blocklist add-on.

subject: JSON contentview key sorting
new: JSON contentview no longer sorts keys
truth: JSON contentview no longer sorts keys; the order of JSON object keys in the display now matches the original response order.
quote: Stop sorting keys in JSON contentview

from_fact: json contentview no longer sorts keys

subject: Clicking URL in mitmweb
old: Clicking the URL in mitmweb selected the entire URL
new: Clicking the URL in mitmweb places the cursor at the current position
truth: Clicking the URL in mitmweb now places the cursor at the current position instead of selecting the entire URL.
quote: Clicking the URL in mitmweb now places the cursor at the current position instead of selecting the entire URL.

from_fact: clicking the url in mitmweb selected the entire url
clicking the url in mitmweb places the cursor at the current position

subject: HTTP detection heuristic
truth: Custom TCP-based protocols that were previously misidentified as HTTP may no longer be intercepted by mitmproxy.
quote: Tighten HTTP detection heuristic to better support custom TCP-based protocols.

subject: HTTP header validation
truth: HTTP requests or responses with malformed headers that were previously accepted will now be rejected or dropped.
quote: Implement stricter validation of HTTP headers to harden against request smuggling attacks.

subject: modify_headers option interaction with stream_large_bodies
old: modify_headers filters could reference the message body
new: modify_headers filters can no longer reference the message body
truth: Filters in the `modify_headers` option that reference the message body will silently stop working.
quote: Fix interaction of the `modify_headers` and `stream_large_bodies` options. This may break users of `modify_headers` that rely on filters referencing the message body.

from_fact: modify_headers filters could reference the message body
modify_headers filters can no longer reference the message body

subject: protobuf dependency
old: protobuf library was available as a dependency
truth: The `protobuf` library is no longer installed as a dependency of mitmproxy; code that imports it must install it separately.
quote: Remove dependency on `protobuf` library as it was no longer being used.

from_fact: protobuf library was available as a dependency

subject: Connection.tls_version
truth: Stop checking Connection.tls_version for the string 'QUIC' on QUIC connections; it now returns 'QUICv1' instead.
quote: `Connection.tls_version` now is `QUICv1` instead of `QUIC` for QUIC.

subject: allow_hosts/ignore_hosts matching
truth: allow_hosts and ignore_hosts options now match against the full host:port string instead of just the hostname.
quote: `allow_hosts`/`ignore_hosts` option now matches against the full `host:port` string.

subject: minimum cryptography version
truth: The minimum required version of the cryptography library is now 42.0; older versions will cause import errors or CryptographyDeprecationWarnings.
quote: This bumps the minimum required version to cryptography 42.0.

subject: UDP stream API
old: UDP traffic exposed as a callback for each packet
truth: Stop using per-packet callbacks for UDP traffic; use stream-based API instead.
quote: UDP streams are now backed by a new implementation in `mitmproxy_rs`. This represents a major API change as UDP traffic is now exposed as streams instead of a callback for each packet.

from_fact: udp traffic exposed as a callback for each packet

subject: Subject Alternative Names representation
truth: Stop using list of strings for Subject Alternative Names; use cryptography.x509.GeneralNames instead.
quote: Subject Alternative Names are now represented as `cryptography.x509.GeneralNames` instead of `list[str]` across the codebase.

subject: keybinding for exporting flow
truth: Stop using 'e' to export a flow; use 'x' instead.
quote: Change keybinding for exporting flow from "e" to "x" to avoid conflict with "edit" keybinding.

subject: script path resolution
truth: Stop using script paths relative to the current working directory; paths are now resolved relative to the config file.
quote: Scripts with relative paths are now loaded relative to the config file and not where the command is ran

subject: macOS distribution format
new: app bundle containing mitmproxy/mitmweb/mitmdump CLI tools
truth: Stop using standalone mitmproxy binaries on macOS; use the new app bundle that contains the CLI tools instead.
quote: Replaced standalone mitmproxy binaries on macOS with an app bundle that contains the mitmproxy/mitmweb/mitmdump CLI tools.

from_fact: app bundle containing mitmproxy/mitmweb/mitmdump cli tools
associative: run mitmproxy from command line mac
how to install mitmproxy on macos

subject: mitmproxy-rs macOS dependency
old: mitmproxy-rs on macOS did not depend on mitmproxy-macos
new: mitmproxy-rs on macOS now depends on mitmproxy-macos, which only provides binary wheels (building from source requires a valid Apple Developer Id)
truth: On macOS, mitmproxy-rs now depends on mitmproxy-macos, which only ships as binary wheels; building from source requires a valid Apple Developer Id.
quote: On macOS, mitmproxy-rs now depends on mitmproxy-macos. We only provide binary wheels for this package because it contains a code-signed system extension. Building from source requires a valid Apple Developer Id, see CI for details.

from_fact: mitmproxy-rs on macos did not depend on mitmproxy-macos
mitmproxy-rs on macos now depends on mitmproxy-macos, which only provides binary wheels (building from source requires a valid apple developer id)

subject: mitmproxy-rs Windows dependency
old: mitmproxy-rs on Windows did not depend on mitmproxy-windows
new: mitmproxy-rs on Windows now depends on mitmproxy-windows, which only provides binary wheels
truth: On Windows, mitmproxy-rs now depends on mitmproxy-windows, which only ships as binary wheels.
quote: On Windows, mitmproxy-rs now depends on mitmproxy-windows. We only provide binary wheels for this package to simplify our deployment process, see CI for how to build from source.

from_fact: mitmproxy-rs on windows did not depend on mitmproxy-windows
mitmproxy-rs on windows now depends on mitmproxy-windows, which only provides binary wheels

subject: string escaping in raw view
truth: Stop relying on string escaping being shown in the raw view; it is now removed.
quote: Remove string escaping in raw view.

subject: Request.port
old: Setting Request.port did not update the Host header
new: Setting Request.port now also updates the Host header if present
truth: Setting Request.port now also updates the Host header if present, matching the behaviour of Request.host.
quote: Updating `Request.port` now also updates the Host header if present. This aligns with `Request.host`, which already does this.

from_fact: setting request.port did not update the host header
setting request.port now also updates the host header if present

subject: server_replay_nopop option
truth: Stop using the server_replay_nopop option; it has been renamed to server_replay_reuse.
quote: The `server_replay_nopop` option has been renamed to `server_replay_reuse` to avoid confusing double-negation.

subject: Python version requirement
truth: mitmproxy now requires Python 3.10 or above; Python 3.9 and earlier are no longer supported.
quote: mitmproxy now requires Python 3.10 or above.

subject: onboarding_port option
truth: Stop using the onboarding_port option; the onboarding app now responds to all requests for the hostname specified in onboarding_host.
quote: The `onboarding_port` option has been removed.

subject: connection.Client and connection.Server constructors
old: connection.Client and connection.Server accepted positional arguments
new: connection.Client and connection.Server now accept keyword arguments only
truth: connection.Client and connection.Server now accept keyword arguments only; stop passing positional arguments to these constructors.
quote: `connection.Client` and `connection.Server` now accept keyword arguments only.

from_fact: connection.client and connection.server accepted positional arguments
connection.client and connection.server now accept keyword arguments only

subject: mode option
old: mode option was a single server spec
new: mode option is now a list of server specs
truth: The mode option is now a list of server specs instead of a single spec; update config.yaml to use a list.
quote: The `mode` option is now a list of server specs instead of a single spec.

from_fact: mode option was a single server spec
mode option is now a list of server specs

subject: connection_strategy option
old: Setting connection_strategy to 'lazy' only deferred upstream connections for some purposes
new: Setting connection_strategy to 'lazy' now also disables early upstream connections to fetch TLS certificate details
truth: Setting connection_strategy to 'lazy' now disables early upstream connections to fetch TLS certificate details, which may break code that relied on those early connections succeeding.
quote: Setting `connection_strategy` to `lazy` now also disables early upstream connections to fetch TLS certificate details.

from_fact: setting connection_strategy to 'lazy' only deferred upstream connections for some purposes
setting connection_strategy to 'lazy' now also disables early upstream connections to fetch tls certificate details

subject: minimum glibc version for Linux binary
new: Linux binary now requires glibc >= 2.31 (Ubuntu 20.04 based)
truth: The Linux binary now requires glibc >= 2.31; running on older systems (e.g. Ubuntu 18.04) will fail with a runtime error.
quote: Update Linux binary builder to Ubuntu 20.04, bumping the minimum glibc version to 2.31.

from_fact: linux binary now requires glibc >= 2.31 (ubuntu 20.04 based)

subject: pkg_resources dependency
truth: Stop using pkg_resources; it has been removed as a dependency.
quote: Drop pkg_resources dependency.

subject: assertions in HTTP state machine
old: overambitious assertions in the HTTP state machine
truth: Assertions in the HTTP state machine have been removed; code that relied on those assertions crashing on invalid state will now proceed differently.
quote: Remove overambitious assertions in the HTTP state machine, fix some error handling.

from_fact: overambitious assertions in the http state machine

subject: Python 3.8 support
truth: Stop using Python 3.8; mitmproxy now requires Python 3.9 or above.
quote: Mitmproxy now requires Python 3.9 or above.

subject: mailcap file handling for external viewers
old: mailcap files were considered when opening an external viewer for message contents
new: mailcap files are not considered anymore when opening an external viewer for message contents
truth: Stop relying on mailcap files being used when opening an external viewer for message contents; they are no longer considered.
quote: When opening an external viewer for message contents, mailcap files are not considered anymore.

from_fact: mailcap files were considered when opening an external viewer for message contents
mailcap files are not considered anymore when opening an external viewer for message contents

subject: connection event hooks
truth: Connection event hooks are now blocking; processing will only resume once the event hook has finished.
quote: Change connection event hooks to be blocking. Processing will only resume once the event hook has finished.

subject: user-added header names in HTTP/2
truth: User-added header names are now lowercased when using HTTP/2, with a log message emitted to notify the user.
quote: Lowercase user-added header names and emit a log message to notify the user when using HTTP/2

subject: events
old: clientconnect, clientdisconnect, serverconnect, serverdisconnect, and log events
truth: Stop using clientconnect, clientdisconnect, serverconnect, serverdisconnect, and log events; use the new replacement events documented in the addon docs.
quote: The `clientconnect`, `clientdisconnect`, `serverconnect`, `serverdisconnect`, and `log` events have been replaced with new events, see addon documentation for details

from_fact: clientconnect, clientdisconnect, serverconnect, serverdisconnect, and log events

subject: ContentViews
truth: Stop implementing should_render on ContentViews; implement render_priority instead.
quote: Contentviews now implement `render_priority` instead of `should_render`, allowing more specialization

subject: legacy protocol stack
truth: The legacy protocol stack has been removed; code depending on it will break.
quote: Remove the legacy protocol stack

subject: pathod and pathoc tools
old: pathod and pathoc tools and modules
truth: All deprecated pathod and pathoc tools and modules have been removed.
quote: Remove all deprecated pathod and pathoc tools and modules

from_fact: pathod and pathoc tools and modules

subject: reverse proxy mode TLS assumption
old: mitmproxy assumed TLS in reverse proxy mode when no scheme was given but a custom port was provided
new: mitmproxy does not assume TLS in reverse proxy mode when no scheme is given but a custom port is provided
truth: In reverse proxy mode, mitmproxy no longer assumes TLS when no scheme is given but a custom port is provided; existing configs relying on that auto-detection will silently connect without TLS.
quote: In reverse proxy mode, mitmproxy now does not assume TLS if no scheme is given but a custom port is provided

from_fact: mitmproxy assumed tls in reverse proxy mode when no scheme was given but a custom port was provided
mitmproxy does not assume tls in reverse proxy mode when no scheme is given but a custom port is provided

subject: HTTP/2 Push Promises support
truth: Support for HTTP/2 Push Promises has been dropped.
quote: Support for HTTP/2 Push Promises has been dropped.

subject: Python 3.7 and below
truth: Stop using Python versions below 3.8; mitmproxy now requires Python 3.8 or above.
quote: Mitmproxy now requires Python 3.8 or above.

subject: pathod and pathoc tools/modules
old: pathod and pathoc tools and modules
truth: Stop using pathod and pathoc; they are deprecated and will be removed in future releases.
quote: Deprecation of pathod and pathoc tools and modules. Future releases will not contain them!

from_fact: pathod and pathoc tools and modules

subject: trailing newline stripping in console
old: console_strip_trailing_newlines default was true (trailing newlines were stripped by default)
new: console_strip_trailing_newlines option added, default is false (trailing newlines are no longer stripped by default)
truth: Stop relying on trailing newlines being stripped from console output; they are now preserved by default unless you set console_strip_trailing_newlines=true.
quote: Add option `console_strip_trailing_newlines`, and no longer strip trailing newlines by default

from_fact: console_strip_trailing_newlines default was true (trailing newlines were stripped by default)
console_strip_trailing_newlines option added, default is false (trailing newlines are no longer stripped by default)

subject: SetHeaders addon
truth: Stop using the SetHeaders addon; use the new ModifyHeaders addon instead.
quote: Rename SetHeaders addon to ModifyHeaders (@mplattner)

subject: mitmweb 'New -> File' menu option
truth: Stop using the 'New -> File' menu option in mitmweb; use the renamed 'Clear All' menu option instead.
quote: mitmweb: "New -> File" menu option has been renamed to "Clear All" (@yogeshojha)

subject: mitmweb's `web_iface` option
truth: Stop using the `web_iface` option; use the renamed `web_host` option instead.
quote: Rename mitmweb's `web_iface` option to `web_host` for consistency (@oxr463)

subject: SIGTERM signal handling
old: SIGTERM asked for confirmation before exiting
truth: Sending SIGTERM to mitmproxy now exits immediately without prompting; SIGINT still asks for confirmation.
quote: Sending a SIGTERM now exits mitmproxy without prompt, SIGINT still asks (@ThinkChaos)

from_fact: sigterm asked for confirmation before exiting

subject: Host header on outgoing requests
old: mitmproxy forced the host header on outgoing requests
new: mitmproxy no longer forces the host header on outgoing requests
truth: mitmproxy no longer forces the host header on outgoing requests, which may break code that relied on the host header being set.
quote: Don't force host header on outgoing requests (@mhils)

from_fact: mitmproxy forced the host header on outgoing requests
mitmproxy no longer forces the host header on outgoing requests

subject: serverconnect event timing
old: serverconnect event was emitted before connection was established
new: serverconnect event is emitted only after connection has been established
truth: The serverconnect event is now emitted only after a connection has been established, not before.
quote: Emit serverconnect event only after a connection has been established (@Prinzhorn)

from_fact: serverconnect event was emitted before connection was established
serverconnect event is emitted only after connection has been established

subject: certificate lifetime
old: certificates valid for longer than 2 years
truth: Generated certificates now have a lifetime of 2 years instead of the previous longer duration; existing certificates may become invalid sooner.
quote: Reduce certificate lifetime to two years to be conformant with the current CA/Browser Forum Baseline Requirements.

from_fact: certificates valid for longer than 2 years

subject: error output
truth: Error messages are now written to sys.stderr instead of sys.stdout; scripts capturing stdout will miss errors.
quote: Errors are now displayed on sys.stderr

subject: config file name
old: config file must be named config.yml
new: config file can be named config.yml or config.yaml
truth: The config file can now be named config.yaml in addition to config.yml; if both exist, behavior may be ambiguous.
quote: Config file can now be called `config.yml` or `config.yaml`

from_fact: config file must be named config.yml
config file can be named config.yml or config.yaml

subject: external editor variable
old: external editor set via unspecified mechanism
new: external editor set via $MITMPROXY_EDITOR environment variable
truth: The external editor is now configured via the $MITMPROXY_EDITOR environment variable; previous editor configuration methods may no longer work.
quote: Users can now set a specific external editor using $MITMPROXY_EDITOR

from_fact: external editor set via unspecified mechanism
external editor set via $mitmproxy_editor environment variable

subject: CI platform support
old: old Linux and macOS releases supported
new: old Linux and macOS releases no longer supported
truth: Old Linux and macOS releases are no longer supported due to migration to GitHub CI.
quote: Moved to GitHub CI for Continuous Integration, dropping support for old Linux and macOS releases.

from_fact: old linux and macos releases supported
old linux and macos releases no longer supported

subject: Python version requirement
truth: mitmproxy now requires Python 3.6 or later; running on Python 3.5 or earlier will fail to start.
quote: mitmproxy now requires Python 3.6!

subject: --conf flag
truth: The --conf flag is now --confdir and specifies the configuration directory instead of the options yaml file directly.
quote: The --conf command-line flag is now --confdir, and specifies the mitmproxy configuration directory, instead of the options yaml file (which is at config.yaml under the configuration directory).

subject: allow_remote option
truth: The allow_remote option has been replaced by block_global and block_private; code using allow_remote will break.
quote: allow_remote got replaced by block_global and block_private (#3100)

subject: custom events
truth: Custom events have been removed entirely; any addon relying on custom events will no longer work.
quote: No more custom events (#3093)

subject: cadir option
truth: The cadir option has been renamed to confdir; code referencing cadir will break.
quote: The cadir option has been renamed to confdir

subject: print statement capture in addons
old: print statements in addons were automatically captured and logged
new: print statements are no longer captured; use ctx.log.info explicitly
truth: Print statements in addons are no longer magically captured and translated to logs; they will silently do nothing visible in logs unless replaced with ctx.log.info.
quote: We no longer magically capture print statements in addons and translate them to logs. Please use ctx.log.info explicitly.

from_fact: print statements in addons were automatically captured and logged
print statements are no longer captured; use ctx.log.info explicitly

subject: HTML outline content view
truth: Stop using the HTML outline content view; it has been removed.
quote: Remove HTML outline contentview (#2572)

subject: Python and Locust export
truth: Stop using the Python and Locust export features; they have been removed.
quote: Remove Python and Locust export (#2465)

subject: WebSockets implementation
old: WebSockets implementation based on previous library
truth: The WebSockets implementation has been migrated to wsproto, which changes the underlying library and adds WebSocket compression support.
quote: Migrate WebSockets implementation to wsproto. This reduces code size and adds WebSocket compression support.

from_fact: websockets implementation based on previous library

subject: Protobuf decoding
old: Protobuf decoding required protoc installed on host
new: Protobuf decoding works without protoc installed
truth: Protobuf decoding no longer requires protoc to be installed on the host system.
quote: Protobuf decoding now works without protoc being installed on the host system

from_fact: protobuf decoding required protoc installed on host
protobuf decoding works without protoc installed

subject: PNG, GIF, JPEG parsing
old: PNG, GIF, JPEG parsing required Pillow (C-based)
new: PNG, GIF, JPEG parsing works without Pillow (pure Python)
truth: PNG, GIF, and JPEG parsing no longer requires Pillow; parsing is now done in pure Python.
quote: PNG, GIF, and JPEG can now be parsed without Pillow, which simplifies mitmproxy installation and moves parsing from unsafe C to pure Python

from_fact: png, gif, jpeg parsing required pillow (c-based)
png, gif, jpeg parsing works without pillow (pure python)

subject: Flow file format versioning
old: Flow file format version tied to mitmproxy version
new: Flow file format version decoupled from mitmproxy version
truth: The flow file format version is now decoupled from the mitmproxy version, which may break compatibility with older flow files.
quote: Decouple mitmproxy version and flow file format version

from_fact: flow file format version tied to mitmproxy version
flow file format version decoupled from mitmproxy version

subject: Script reloading mechanism
truth: Script reloading now polls the filesystem instead of using watchdog, which changes reload behavior and reliability.
quote: Improve script reloading reliability by polling the filesystem instead of using watchdog

subject: stdout in scripts
new: stdout in scripts redirected to ctx.log.warn
truth: Stdout in scripts is now redirected to ctx.log.warn instead of printing directly.
quote: Redirect stdout in scripts to ctx.log.warn

from_fact: stdout in scripts redirected to ctx.log.warn

subject: ~d and ~u filter matching
truth: The ~d and ~u filters now match against pretty_host instead of host, which changes which flows are matched.
quote: Match ~d and ~u filters against pretty_host

subject: Private key removal from PKCS12
old: Private key was included in PKCS12 file in ~/.mitmproxy
new: Private key removed from PKCS12 file in ~/.mitmproxy
truth: The private key is no longer included in the PKCS12 file in ~/.mitmproxy, which may break setups that relied on it being there.
quote: Remove private key from PKCS12 file in ~/.mitmproxy

from_fact: private key was included in pkcs12 file in ~/.mitmproxy
private key removed from pkcs12 file in ~/.mitmproxy

subject: pyOpenSSL, cryptography, OpenSSL versions
old: Older versions of pyOpenSSL, cryptography, OpenSSL
truth: Updated to pyOpenSSL 17.5, cryptography 2.1.4, and OpenSSL 1.1.0g, which may break code relying on older APIs.
quote: Update to pyOpenSSL 17.5, cryptography 2.1.4, and OpenSSL 1.1.0g

from_fact: older versions of pyopenssl, cryptography, openssl

subject: mitmweb React version
truth: mitmweb has been updated to React 16, which may break custom mitmweb extensions or themes.
quote: Update mitmweb to React 16, which brings performance improvements

subject: IPv6 IP address formatting
truth: IPv6 IP address formatting has been fixed, which may break code that relied on the old (incorrect) formatting.
quote: Fix formatting of (IPv6) IP addresses in a number of places

subject: mitmproxy --version output
truth: The output of 'mitmproxy --version' has been simplified, which may break scripts parsing the version string.
quote: Simplify output of mitmproxy --version

subject: Pathoc CONNECT host header
old: Pathoc did not include host header on CONNECT requests
new: Pathoc includes a host header on CONNECT requests
truth: Pathoc now includes a host header on CONNECT requests, which may break tests or scripts that relied on the old behavior.
quote: Pathoc: Include a host header on CONNECT requests

from_fact: pathoc did not include host header on connect requests
pathoc includes a host header on connect requests

subject: Error handler on HTTPException
old: Error handler was not called on HTTPException
new: Error handler is called on HTTPException
truth: The error handler is now called on HTTPException, which may change error handling behavior in addons.
quote: Call error handler on HTTPException

from_fact: error handler was not called on httpexception
error handler is called on httpexception

subject: TLS removal from upstream HTTP connections
old: Could not remove TLS from upstream HTTP connections
new: Can now remove TLS from upstream HTTP connections
truth: It is now possible to remove TLS from upstream HTTP connections, which changes the default behavior for upstream connections.
quote: Make it possible to remove TLS from upstream HTTP connections

from_fact: could not remove tls from upstream http connections
can now remove tls from upstream http connections

subject: Log verbosity retroactive increase
old: Could not retroactively increase log verbosity
truth: It is now possible to retroactively increase log verbosity, which changes logging behavior.
quote: Make it possible to retroactively increase log verbosity.

from_fact: could not retroactively increase log verbosity

subject: Logging thread safety
old: Logging from addons was not thread-safe
new: Logging from addons is now thread-safe
truth: Logging from addons is now thread-safe, which may change the order or timing of log messages.
quote: Make logging from addons thread-safe

from_fact: logging from addons was not thread-safe
logging from addons is now thread-safe

subject: Import tolerance in user scripts
old: Imports matching hook names in user scripts caused errors
new: Imports matching hook names in user scripts are tolerated
truth: User scripts can now import names that match hook names (e.g. 'from mitmproxy import log') without errors, which changes import resolution behavior.
quote: Tolerate imports in user scripts that match hook names (`from mitmproxy import log`)

from_fact: imports matching hook names in user scripts caused errors
imports matching hook names in user scripts are tolerated

subject: Reverting duplicated flows crash fix
new: Reverting duplicated flows no longer crashes
truth: The crash when reverting duplicated flows has been fixed, which changes the behavior of flow reversion.
quote: Fix a bug where reverting duplicated flows crashes mitmproxy

from_fact: reverting duplicated flows no longer crashes

subject: Successive requests after redirect
old: Successive requests went to wrong host after redirect
new: Successive requests go to correct host after redirect
truth: Successive requests after a redirect now go to the correct host, which changes behavior for redirected flows.
quote: Fix a bug where successive requests are sent to the wrong host after a request has been redirected.

from_fact: successive requests went to wrong host after redirect
successive requests go to correct host after redirect

subject: Outgoing connection binding
old: Outgoing connections bound to wrong interface
new: Outgoing connections bind to correct interface
truth: Outgoing connections now bind to the correct interface, which changes network routing behavior.
quote: Fix a bug that binds outgoing connections to the wrong interface

outgoing connections bind to correct interface

subject: Custom certificates in reverse proxy
old: Custom certificates were ignored in reverse proxy mode
new: Custom certificates are no longer ignored in reverse proxy mode
truth: Custom certificates are now respected in reverse proxy mode, which changes TLS behavior for reverse proxy setups.
quote: Fix a bug where custom certificates are ignored in reverse proxy mode

custom certificates are no longer ignored in reverse proxy mode

subject: Import of old flows (0.17)
old: Flows from mitmproxy 0.17 could not be imported
new: Flows from mitmproxy 0.17 can now be imported
truth: Flows created with mitmproxy 0.17 can now be imported, which changes flow import compatibility.
quote: Fix import of flows that have been created with mitmproxy 0.17

flows from mitmproxy 0.17 can now be imported

subject: HTTP/2 flow replay
truth: HTTP/2 flow replay now works correctly, which changes replay behavior for HTTP/2 flows.
quote: Fix replay for HTTP/2 flows

subject: Curl export crash
new: Exporting flows to curl no longer crashes
truth: Exporting flows to curl no longer crashes, which changes export behavior.
quote: Fix a crash when exporting flows to curl

subject: Set-Cookie refresh crash
new: Refreshing Set-Cookie headers no longer crashes

quote: Fix a crash when refreshing Set-Cookie headers

subject: Connection indicator in mitmweb
truth: A connection indicator has been added to mitmweb to alert when the proxy server stops running, which changes the UI.
quote: Add connection indicator to mitmweb to alert users when the proxy server stops running

subject: Cyrillic domain certificates
old: Certificates with Cyrillic domains were not supported
new: Certificates with Cyrillic domains are now supported
truth: Support for certificates with Cyrillic domains has been added, which changes certificate handling for non-ASCII domains.
quote: Add support for certificates with cyrillic domains

certificates with cyrillic domains are now supported

subject: Request.make method
truth: A new Request.make method has been added to simplify request creation in scripts, which changes the API for creating requests.
quote: Add Request.make to simplify request creation in scripts

subject: Flow certificate storage
old: Generated certificate not stored per flow
new: Generated certificate stored for each flow
truth: The generated certificate is now stored for each flow, which changes flow metadata and storage.

old: HEAD requests for mitm.it not handled
new: HEAD requests for mitm.it are handled

old: DNS spoofing example did not use --keep-host-header
new: DNS spoofing example updated to use --keep-host-header

old: Transparent mode broken on IPv6-enabled macOS
new: Transparent mode works on IPv6-enabled macOS

new: Testsuite works when path contains a '.'

truth: Filters for WebSocket flows have been added, which changes flow filtering behavior.

subject: Toggle interception
truth: The ability to toggle interception has been added, which changes interception behavior.
quote: Add ability to toggle interception

subject: LDAP proxy authentication
truth: LDAP has been added as a proxy authentication backend, which changes authentication options.
quote: Add LDAP as a proxy authentication backend

subject: Keep host header in reverse proxy

truth: An options editor has been added to mitmweb, which changes the mitmweb UI and configuration interface.

old: No split view in mitmproxy UI

old: No key binding viewer and editor

old: Could not spawn Chrome from mitmproxy

subject: WSL support
old: mitmproxy had display errors on WSL
truth: mitmproxy now fully supports Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) with display errors worked around, which changes behavior on WSL.

from_fact: mitmproxy had display errors on wsl

subject: XSS scanner addon
truth: An XSS scanner addon has been added, which changes addon availability.
quote: Add XSS scanner addon

subject: Certificate installation on mitm.it
old: No certificate installation instructions on mitm.it
truth: Certificate installation instructions are now provided on mitm.it, which changes the mitm.it behavior.
quote: Provide certificate installation instructions on mitm.it

from_fact: no certificate installation instructions on mitm.it

subject: HTTP/2 default state
truth: HTTP/2 is now enabled by default; existing setups relying on HTTP/2 being off will now use HTTP/2 unless explicitly disabled.
quote: HTTP/2 is now enabled by default.

subject: Image ContentView parser
truth: Image ContentView now uses Kaitai Struct instead of Pillow; any code or custom content viewers depending on Pillow-based image parsing will break.
quote: Image ContentView: Parse images with Kaitai Struct (kaitai.io) instead of Pillow.

subject: Python 2 support
truth: All mitmproxy tools are now Python 3 only (3.5+); any scripts or deployments running on Python 2 will no longer work.
quote: All mitmproxy tools are now Python 3 only! We plan to support Python 3.5 and higher.

subject: Configuration file format
truth: The config file format is now a single YAML file; existing config files in the old format will not be read and must be converted.
quote: Configuration: The config file format is now a single YAML file. In most cases, converting to the new format should be trivial - please see the docs for more information.

subject: HTTP/2 default state (0.18 -> 1.0)
truth: HTTP/2 is temporarily disabled by default due to widespread protocol implementation errors; existing setups relying on HTTP/2 being on will silently fall back to HTTP/1.1.
quote: HTTP/2: Significant improvements, but is temporarily disabled by default due to wide-spread protocol implementation errors on some large website

subject: Python package name
truth: Stop importing from libmproxy; import from mitmproxy instead.
quote: Rename the Python package from libmproxy to mitmproxy.

subject: HTTP message body attributes
old: raw_content, content, text had different/undefined semantics
new: .raw_content = body as seen on wire, .content = decompressed body, .text = decompressed and decoded body
truth: Use .raw_content for wire-level body, .content for decompressed body, and .text for decompressed+decoded body; existing code relying on previous semantics will silently get different data.
quote: Improved message body handling for HTTP messages: .raw_content provides the message body as seen on the wire .content provides the decompressed body (e.g. un-gzipped) .text provides the body decompressed and decoded body

from_fact: raw_content, content, text had different/undefined semantics
.raw_content = body as seen on wire, .content = decompressed body, .text = decompressed and decoded body

subject: certforward feature
truth: Stop using the certforward feature; it has been removed entirely.
quote: Remove the certforward feature. It was added to allow exploitation of #gotofail, which is no longer a common vulnerability. Permitting this hugely increased the complexity of packaging and distributing mitmproxy.

subject: mitmproxy console keyboard shortcuts g/G
new: g/G keyboard shortcuts now match less
truth: The g and G keyboard shortcuts in mitmproxy console have changed to match less behaviour; existing muscle memory for these keys will break.
quote: mitmproxy console: change g/G keyboard shortcuts to match less. Thanks to Jose Luis Honorato (github.com/jlhonora).

from_fact: g/g keyboard shortcuts now match less

subject: crypto defaults
old: previous crypto defaults (SHA1, 1024 bit RSA, 1024 bit DH)
new: SHA256, 2048 bit RSA, 4096 bit DH parameters
truth: Crypto defaults changed to SHA256, 2048-bit RSA, and 4096-bit DH parameters; existing hardcoded assumptions about key sizes or hash algorithms will break.
quote: Update our crypto defaults: SHA256, 2048 bit RSA, 4096 bit DH parameters.

from_fact: previous crypto defaults (sha1, 1024 bit rsa, 1024 bit dh)
sha256, 2048 bit rsa, 4096 bit dh parameters

subject: mitmproxy console keybindings for options
old: Options previously manipulated directly via keybindings
truth: Stop using keybindings to manipulate options directly; use the new options screen ('o' shortcut) instead.
quote: A new options screen has been created ("o" shortcut), and many options that were previously manipulated directly via a keybinding have been moved there.

from_fact: options previously manipulated directly via keybindings

subject: SSL certificate storage
truth: Interception certificates are now stored in-memory instead of on disk, which fixes a race condition and improves Windows compatibility but may affect workflows that relied on certificate files on disk.
quote: Revamp the way we store interception certificates. We used to store these on disk, they're now in-memory. This fixes a race condition related to cert handling, and improves compatibility with Windows, where the rules governing permitted file names are weird, resulting in errors for some valid IDNA-encoded names.

subject: upstream certs mode
old: upstream certs mode was optional/disabled by default
new: upstream certs mode is now the default
truth: Upstream certs mode is now enabled by default, which changes how upstream certificates are handled and may break setups that relied on the previous default behaviour.
quote: Upstream certs mode is now the default.

from_fact: upstream certs mode was optional/disabled by default
upstream certs mode is now the default

subject: serialization format
truth: Saved traffic from mitmproxy 0.8 is not compatible with 0.7 due to server certificates now being stored and serialized.
quote: Server certificates are now stored and serialized in saved traffic for later analysis. This means that the 0.8 serialization format is NOT compatible with 0.7.

subject: serialization format
truth: Serialized data from mitmproxy 0.6 is not compatible with 0.7 due to the move to typed netstrings.
quote: Move to typed netstrings for serialization format - this makes 0.7 backwards-incompatible with serialized data from 0.6!

subject: mitmproxy command-line options
old: mitmproxy and mitmdump had different/unregularized command-line options
new: mitmproxy and mitmdump now have regularized command-line options
truth: Command-line options for mitmproxy and mitmdump have been regularized, so existing scripts using old option names or formats may break.
quote: Regularize command-line options for mitmproxy and mitmdump.

from_fact: mitmproxy and mitmdump had different/unregularized command-line options
mitmproxy and mitmdump now have regularized command-line options

subject: large body rendering in mitmproxy
old: mitmproxy rendered entire request/response bodies regardless of size
new: mitmproxy only renders the first 100k of large documents and avoids running XML indenter on non-XML data
truth: Viewing large request/response bodies now truncates at 100k and skips XML indentation for non-XML data, so full body content is no longer displayed.
quote: Handle viewing big request and response bodies more elegantly. Only render the first 100k of large documents, and try to avoid running the XML indenter on non-XML data.

from_fact: mitmproxy rendered entire request/response bodies regardless of size
mitmproxy only renders the first 100k of large documents and avoids running xml indenter on non-xml data
